{
  "claims": [
    "According to PG&E, natural gas residential customers will receive around $53.",
    "Residential electric customers will receive approximately $38.",
    "Customers who receive both gas and electric services will receive a total credit of $91.",
    "More than 300,000 eligible PG&E customers who experienced financial hardships during the pandemic will receive an automatic one-time bill credit in early February under the California Arrearage Payment Program.",
    "The recent increase in natural gas prices was a significant factor in why utility bills rose throughout the country.",
    "Many sections of the U.S. pipeline system had to undergo repairs, which contributed to the rise in utility bills.",
    "Natural gas exports from Europe decreased due to the war in Ukraine, impacting prices.",
    "Energy prices skyrocketed more than 300 percent from last year."
  ]
}
